texto: She was the lover of two mighty kings.She was their Chalice.The certainty of the knowledge settled over her shoulders like a weighty mantle. She might not know where she came from, but she knew where she was going. She knew who she wanted to be, who she was.She was the queen of Amendal, leader and protector of the dal.Time to start acting the part. She stood and pulled them up with her. Amendal was still in danger, and she would not stand for it any longer.By Aiea, and all the gods of Odren, no more would die today.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Elizabeth Varlet: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “She was the lover of two mighty kings.She was their Chalice.The certainty of the knowledge settle…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, epic-fantasy, fantasy pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e fantasy.

frase_original: She was the lover of two mighty kings.She was their Chalice.The certainty of the knowledge settled over her shoulders like a weighty mantle. She might not know where she came from, but she knew where she was going. She knew who she wanted to be, who she was.She was the queen of Amendal, leader and protector of the dal.Time to start acting the part. She stood and pulled them up with her. Amendal was still in danger, and she would not stand for it any longer.By Aiea, and all the gods of Odren, no more would die today.
